why: The predeclared whole-box colour check and an author-pilot judgement disagree. This post-hoc diagnostic investigates sensitivity to evaluation support; it cannot determine which verdict is correct.
finding: The whole declared box gives dE 36.1, while the output-derived changed-pixel patch gives dE 21.3. The verdict is support-sensitive.
consequence: Grounding affects both the pixels written and the region measured. Because the changed-pixel mask is derived from the output, it is circular as a primary evaluation support and is retained only as a post-hoc diagnostic.
caveats: The dE threshold of 25 is hand-set and uncalibrated. The changed-pixel mask violates the experiment's predeclared-support rule. The patch mean is darker than the requested tan, material remains unverified, and the human evidence is one hypothesis-aware author. Semantic acceptability remains unresolved.
how_it_surfaced: The author's side-obscured Q5 answer (YES) contradicted the earlier report and prompted this sensitivity check.
